revision.text:
[[ar:أموغاسيدهي]][[bg:Амогхасидхи]][[bn:অমোঘসিদ্ধি]][[bo:དོན་གྲུབ།]][[cs:Amóghasiddhi]][[de:Amoghasiddhi]][[en:Amoghasiddhi]][[es:Amoghasiddhi]][[et:Amoghasiddhi]][[fr:Amoghasiddhi]][[hi:अमोघसिद्धि]][[hu:Amóghasziddhi]][[id:Amoghasiddhi]][[it:Amogjasiddhi]][[ja:不空成就如来]][[ka:ამოგჰასიდჰი]][[lt:Amoghasidhis]][[nl:Amoghasiddhi]][[no:Amoghasiddhi]][[pl:Amoghasiddhi]][[pnb:اموگھاسدھی]][[pt:Amoghasiddhi]][[ro:Amoghasiddhi]][[ru:Амогхасиддхи]][[sv:Amoghasiddhi]][[ta:அமோகசித்தி புத்தர்]][[th:พระอโมฆสิทธิพุทธะ]][[uk:Амогхасіддхи]][[ur:اموگھاسدھی]][[zh-yue:不空成就如來]][[zh:不空成就佛]] {{Short description|Buddha of wisdom in Tibetan Buddhism|qq=}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Amoghasiddhi | image = COLLECTIE TROPENMUSEUM Boeddhabeeld van de Borobudur voorstellende Dhyani Boeddha Amogasiddha TMnr 10025273.jpg | image_caption = Ancient sculpture of Amoghasiddhi Buddha. {{ill|Borobudur|en|Borobudur|qid=Q42798|lt=Borobudur|1=Borobudur}}, {{ill|Central Java|en|Central Java|qid=Q3557|lt=Central Java|1=Central Java}}, {{ill|Indonesia|en|Indonesia|qid=Q252|lt=Indonesia|1=Indonesia}} | sanskrit_name = अमोघसिद्धि
Amoghasiddhi | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 不空成就佛
({{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|qid=Q42222|lt=Pinyin|1=Pinyin}}: ''Bùkōngchéngjiù Fó'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|不空成就仏|ふくうじょうじゅぶつ|qq=}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Fukūjōju Butsu'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = អមោឃសិទ្ធិ
([[Romanization of Khmer#UNGEGN|UNGEGN]]: {{transl|km|Âmoŭkhôsĕtthĭ|qq=}}) | korean_name = 불공성취불
({{ill|Revised Romanization of Korean|en|Revised Romanization of Korean|qid=Q498721|lt=RR|1=Revised Romanization of Korean}}: ''Bulgongseongchwi Bosal'') | mongolian_name = ᠲᠡᠭᠦᠰ ᠨᠥᠭᠴᠢᠭᠰᠡᠨ
Үйлс бүтээгч
([[SASM/GNC romanization|SASM/GNC]]: ''Tegüs nögcigsen'') | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | thai_name = พระอโมฆสิทธิพุทธะ
Phraamoksitthiphuttha | tibetan_name = དོན་ཡོད་གྲུབ་པ་
Wylie: don yod grub pa
THL: dönyö drubpa | vietnamese_name = Bất Không Thành Tựu Phật | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] |caption=Statue of Amogasiddha from {{ill|Borobudur|en|Borobudur|qid=Q42798|lt=Borobudur|1=Borobudur}}, {{ill|Central Java|en|Central Java|qid=Q3557|lt=Central Java|1=Central Java}}, {{ill|Indonesia|en|Indonesia|qid=Q252|lt=Indonesia|1=Indonesia}}|qq=}} '''Amoghasiddhi''' ({{ill|Devanagari|en|Devanagari|qid=Q38592|lt=Devanagari|1=Devanagari}}: अमोघसिद्धि) is one of the [[:en:Five Wisdom Buddhas|Five Wisdom Buddhas]] of the {{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|qid=Q48362|lt=Mahayana|1=Mahayana}} and {{ill|Vajrayana|en|Vajrayana|qid=Q172175|lt=Vajrayana|1=Vajrayana}} tradition of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}. He is associated with the accomplishment of the Buddhist path and of the destruction of the poison of {{ill|envy|en|envy|qid=Q188739|lt=envy|1=Envy}}. His name means ''Unfailing Accomplishment''. His consort is {{ill|Tara (Buddhism)|en|Tara (Buddhism)|qid=Q746709|lt=Tara|1=Tara (Buddhism)}}, meaning ''Liberator'' and his mounts are {{ill|Garuda|en|Garuda|qid=Q188676|lt=Garuda|1=Garuda}}s. He belongs to the family of {{ill|Karma|en|Karma|qid=Q132196|lt=Karma|1=Karma}} whose family symbol is the double ''vajra''.[http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif Double Dorje] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090121225526/http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif |date=January 21, 2009 |qq=}}{{cite web|url=http://www.religionfacts.com/buddhism/deities/five_dhyani_buddhas.htm |title=The Five Dhyani Buddhas (Great Buddhas of Wisdom) |publisher=Religionfacts.com |date=2012-12-21 |accessdate=2013-06-14|qq=}} == Characteristics == Amoghasiddhi is associated with the conceptual (Skt: {{ill|Samskara (Indian philosophy)|en|Samskara (Indian philosophy)|qid=Q2618188|lt=samskara|1=Samskara (Indian philosophy)}}) {{ill|Skandha|en|Skandha|qid=Q747858|lt=Skandha|1=Skandha}} or the conceptual mind (as opposed to the non-conceptual or sensational mind). His action towards the promotion of Buddhist paths is the pacification of evils. This is symbolised by Amoghasiddhi's symbol, the moon. He gestures in the {{ill|Mudra|en|Mudra|qid=Q208030|lt=Mudra|1=Mudra}} of fearlessness, symbolising his and his devotees' fearlessness towards the poisons or delusions. He is usually coloured green in artwork and is associated with the {{ill|Classical element|en|Classical element|qid=Q188520|lt=air or wind|1=Classical element}} element. His season is autumn and his heavenly quarter is the northern buddha-kṣetra called Prakuta. In the [[:en:Shurangama Mantra|Śūraṅgama mantra]] ({{ill|Chinese language|en|Chinese language|qid=Q7850|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese language}}: 楞嚴咒; {{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|qid=Q42222|lt=Pinyin|1=Pinyin}}: ''Léngyán Zhòu'') taught in the {{ill|Śūraṅgama Sūtra|en|Śūraṅgama Sūtra|qid=Q2176806|lt=Śūraṅgama sutra|1=Śūraṅgama Sūtra}} ({{ill|Chinese language|en|Chinese language|qid=Q7850|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese language}}: 楞嚴經; {{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|qid=Q42222|lt=Pinyin|1=Pinyin}}: ''Léngyán Jīng''), an especially influential {{ill|Dharani|en|Dharani|qid=Q1207590|lt=Dharani|1=Dharani}} in the {{ill|Chan Buddhism|en|Chan Buddhism|qid=Q940709|lt=Chinese Chan tradition|1=Chan Buddhism}}, Amoghasiddhi is mentioned to be the host of the Karma Division in the North, one of the five major divisions which controls the vast demon armies of the five directions.{{Cite book|url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/300721049|title=The Śūraṅgama sūtra : a new translation|date=2009|publisher=Buddhist Text Translation Society|others=Hsüan Hua, Buddhist Text Translation Society|isbn=978-0-88139-962-2|location=Ukiah, Calif.|oclc=300721049|qq=}} == Gallery == File:Jin Dynasty statue of Amoghasiddhi (不空成就佛 Bùkōngchéngjiùfó), one of the Five Tathagathas (五方佛 Wǔfāngfó) or Five Wisdom Buddhas (五智如来 Wǔzhì Rúlái) at Shanhua Temple (善化寺) in Datong, Shanxi, China.jpg|{{ill|Jin dynasty (1115–1234)|en|Jin dynasty (1115–1234)|qid=Q5066|lt=Jin dynasty|1=Jin dynasty (1115–1234)}} (1115–1234) statue of Amoghasiddhi in {{ill|Shanhua Temple|en|Shanhua Temple|qid=Q548433|lt=Shanhua Temple|1=Shanhua Temple}} in {{ill|Datong|en|Datong|qid=Q72877|lt=Datong|1=Datong}}, {{ill|Shanxi|en|Shanxi|qid=Q46913|lt=Shanxi|1=Shanxi}}, {{ill|China|en|China|qid=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}, one out of a set of statues of the {{ill|Five Tathāgatas|en|Five Tathāgatas|qid=Q265911|lt=Five Tathagathas|1=Five Tathāgatas}} File:Ming era statue of Amoghasiddhi (不空成就佛 Bùkōngchéngjiùfó), one of the Five Tathagathas (五方佛 Wǔfāngfó) or Five Wisdom Buddhas (五智如来 Wǔzhì Rúlái) at Huayan Temple (華嚴寺 or 华严寺), Shanxi, China.jpg|{{ill|Ming dynasty|en|Ming dynasty|qid=Q9903|lt=Ming dynasty|1=Ming dynasty}} (1368–1644) statue of Amoghasiddhi in {{ill|Huayan Temple (Datong)|en|Huayan Temple (Datong)|qid=Q15932853|lt=Huayan Temple|1=Huayan Temple (Datong)}} in {{ill|Datong|en|Datong|qid=Q72877|lt=Datong|1=Datong}}, {{ill|Shanxi|en|Shanxi|qid=Q46913|lt=Shanxi|1=Shanxi}}, {{ill|China|en|China|qid=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}, one out of a set of statues of the {{ill|Five Tathāgatas|en|Five Tathāgatas|qid=Q265911|lt=Five Tathagathas|1=Five Tathāgatas}} File:Periodo yongle nella din. ming, amoghasiddhi, uno dei 5 buddha celestiali, seduto nel gesto di meditazione e rassicurazione, 1403-24 ca.jpg|{{ill|Ming dynasty|en|Ming dynasty|qid=Q9903|lt=Ming dynasty|1=Ming dynasty}} figurine of Amoghasiddhi, made during the reign of the {{ill|Yongle Emperor|en|Yongle Emperor|qid=Q9965|lt=Yongle Emperor|1=Yongle Emperor}}, early 1400s File:蓮華院多宝塔の不空成就如来.jpg|Modern statue of Amoghasiddhi in {{ill|Renge-in Tanjō-ji|en|Renge-in Tanjō-ji|qid=Q11621755|lt=Renge-in Tanjō-ji|1=Renge-in Tanjō-ji}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. File:Amoghasiddhi1.jpg|Amoghasiddhi, depicted with green skin File:Tibetan Amoghasiddhi Buddha.jpg|Close up view of Amoghasiddhi from a Tibetan painting, 1300–1400 CE ==See also== * {{ill|Five Tathāgatas|en|Five Tathāgatas|qid=Q265911|lt=Five Tathāgatas|1=Five Tathāgatas}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} ==Further reading== *''Mythology of India: Myths of India, Sri Lanka and Tibet'', Rachel Storm, Anness Publishing Limited, Editor Helen Sudell, Page 15, Column 2–4, Line 5, Caption, Page 15, Column 4, Lines 1 – 5 ==External links== *[http://viewonbuddhism.org/5_dhyani_buddhas.html Symbolism of the five Dhyani Buddhas] *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/101557/rec/1 Sacred Visions: Early Paintings from Central Tibet], an exhibition catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Amoghasiddhi(see index) {{commons Category-inline|qq=}} {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon|qq=}} {{Buddhism topics|qq=}} [[en:Amoghasiddhi]] [[ar:أموغاسيدهي]] [[bg:Амогхасидхи]] [[bn:অমোঘসিদ্ধি]] [[bo:དོན་གྲུབ།]] {{Commons category|Amoghasiddhi}} [[cs:Amóghasiddhi]] [[de:Amoghasiddhi]] [[es:Amoghasiddhi]] [[et:Amoghasiddhi]] [[fr:Amoghasiddhi]] [[hi:अमोघसिद्धि]] [[hu:Amóghasziddhi]] [[id:Amoghasiddhi]] [[it:Amogjasiddhi]] [[ja:不空成就如来]] [[ka:ამოგჰასიდჰი]] [[lt:Amoghasidhis]] [[nl:Amoghasiddhi]] [[no:Amoghasiddhi]] [[pl:Amoghasiddhi]] [[pnb:اموگھاسدھی]] [[pt:Amoghasiddhi]] [[ro:Amoghasiddhi]] [[ru:Амогхасиддхи]] [[sv:Amoghasiddhi]] [[ta:அமோகசித்தி புத்தர்]] [[th:พระอโมฆสิทธิพุทธะ]] [[uk:Амогхасіддхи]] [[ur:اموگھاسدھی]] [[zh:不空成就佛]] [[zh-yue:不空成就如來]] {{wikidata link|Q597586}} [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
